name: generate_illustration
description: Generate a children's book illustration using the Nano Banana 2 image model. Accepts a text prompt describing the desired illustration and returns the file path and metadata of the generated image. The image is saved to disk on the server running nanobanana-image-tool.

prompt.description: A detailed description of the illustration to generate. Be specific about subjects, setting, mood, and visual style. Example: 'a friendly robot waving hello in a colorful town, soft watercolor style, children's book illustration'

negative_prompt.description: Things to avoid in the generated image. Example: 'scary, dark, violent, realistic photography'

width.description: Image width in pixels. Maps to model resolution tiers: <=512 -> 0.5K, <=1024 -> 1K, <=2048 -> 2K, >2048 -> 4K.

height.description: Image height in pixels. Maps to model resolution tiers: <=512 -> 0.5K, <=1024 -> 1K, <=2048 -> 2K, >2048 -> 4K.

seed.description: Random seed for deterministic generation. Omit for a random seed. Use the same seed to reproduce an identical image.

style.description: Visual style preset to apply. Examples: 'watercolor', 'crayon', 'digital illustration', 'pencil sketch', 'pastel'.

output_dir.description: Directory where the generated image will be saved. Defaults to the server's configured output directory.

reference_names.description: Names of previously created character references to include for visual consistency. Create references with the CLI: nanobanana-tool generate-reference --name <name> --prompt <description>.
